The Morning Ritual Blog

Why I Stopped Measuring My Coffee

For years I weighed every gram of coffee, timed every pour, chased the perfect extraction. Then one Tuesday the scale broke, and something shifted. I started making coffee by feel instead — a scoop here, a splash there, judged by smell and color rather than numbers.

This isn't a recipe. There's no ingredient list, no ratio, no method to follow. It's just a story about letting go of precision in one small corner of my life, and what I noticed once I did.

Maybe you have a version of this too — some ritual you've over-optimized past the point of joy. I'd love to hear about it in the comments.
